task:
Fix ticket 0080 — reconcile stale CURRENT-TRUTH "worktree" claims to the active-checkout-only spine (ADR-0023 Amendment 2, founder directive 2026-06-15: the opt-in isolation lane / §4 is REMOVED; there is exactly ONE mode — the spine works on the active checkout / active branch, with NO worktree, NO run branch, NO landing step).

WRITE-SCOPE (hard): cocoder/decisions/** ONLY. Do not edit ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/**, or packages/personas/base/** in this atom — every worktree hit in those paths classified KEEP below. If your own grep classification finds a genuine current-truth contradiction outside cocoder/decisions/**, do NOT edit it — list it in your report for Oscar to route separately (base persona/standards changes are base-governance, a different lane).

STEP 1 — Re-derive, do not trust this list blindly. Run: grep -rni 'worktree' ARCHITECTURE.md cocoder/decisions cocoder/standards cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md docs packages/personas/base . Confirm the site set matches what is below; if a new hit appears, classify it with the same framework.

STEP 2 — Classify EVERY hit as (A) current-truth contradiction = a claim that LIVE runs operate in a worktree / that an opt-in worktree lane currently exists, vs (B) KEEP = correct historical record, retired-lane history with a pointer, storage-dir reference (local/worktrees/ holds pre-2026-06-15 historical runs), kept git/harness worktree PRIMITIVES/capability, generic 'working tree' usage, or an already-correct current-truth statement. Produce this classification table in your final report (file:line | A or B | one-line reason) covering all hits.

FIX (class A — these are the targets):
1. cocoder/decisions/0016-deb-scoped-repair-fallback.md — §3 (~line 59) says Deb edits 'in the run's worktree' as current behavior, and the reconciliation note (~lines 7-10, dated 2026-06-14, PRE-Amendment-2) routes the body's worktree refs to 'the opt-in isolation lane only' — but Amendment 2 REMOVED that lane. Fix by ADDING an 'Amendment 2 (2026-06-15)' reconciliation note in the SAME stacked-note style already used at the top of this file (there is already an Amendment 1 note), stating: the opt-in isolation lane referenced by the body and by the 2026-06-14 note is removed; in the single mode Deb's repair edits the ACTIVE CHECKOUT (no worktree) and commits in place via the spine. Preserve the §3 body decision text as historical record — do not rewrite line 59; the new note supersedes it.
2. cocoder/decisions/0034-retire-adr0015-merge-machinery.md (~line 15-16): 'isolation is an opt-in worktree lane rather than a merge-back invariant' is stated as current — stale post-Amendment-2. Reconcile with an inline superseded-pointer noting ADR-0023 Amendment 2 removed the opt-in lane (single mode, active-checkout-only). NOTE the kept-primitives sentences (~lines 43,45,53: worktreeAdd/worktreeRemove/listWorktrees, git-worktree.test.ts) are class B / KEEP — they are capability, not the lane; do not delete them. If line 45-46 justifies keeping them as 'ADR-0023 §4 isolation mechanics' (a retired section), adjust only that justification to 'kept as git/harness primitives' without claiming a live isolation lane.
3. cocoder/decisions/0041-orchestration-ownership-and-actor-authority.md (~line 240, Alternatives considered): 'Worktree isolation per lane (ADR-0023 opt-in)' treats the opt-in lane as existing. Add an inline pointer that ADR-0023 Amendment 2 removed it; keep the alternative's reasoning intact.
4. cocoder/decisions/0042-run-concurrency-model.md (lines 5, 18, 34): premise repeatedly treats 'opt-in worktree lane' as live current behavior (Builds-on line 5; Context line 18; Tier-3 line 34 're-activating ADR-0023's opt-in worktree lane'). Reconcile each with an inline pointer that Amendment 2 removed the opt-in lane — so line 34 reads as RE-INTRODUCING per-run isolation, not re-activating an existing lane. The concurrency DECISION (per-workspace today / defer Tier-3 to v2) is unchanged; only the stale premise phrasing is corrected.

KEEP (class B — DO NOT change, but include in your classification table with the reason):
- ARCHITECTURE.md:32,52,244 (local-zone storage dir / 'worktrees/ only holds pre-2026-06-15 historical runs'); ARCHITECTURE.md:81 (already the CORRECT current-truth: 'no worktree, no run branch').
- cocoder/decisions/0008:16,34 (storage dir); 0027:100 (explicitly 'obsolete run.worktree_path'); 0016:21 (Builds-on ref to archived ADR-0015), 0016:69 (fingerprint normalization strips 'worktree paths' — capability); 0020:142 ('no worktree needed' — correct current-truth); 0023 ALL hits (its OWN ADR text; §4 is already struck by the Amendment 2 banner at the top — preserve the body as history, do not rewrite); 0042:6 Revisits-line ref to archived 0015 if present.
- docs/** all hits (audits, glossary storage, generic working-tree, the ticket-0080 reference) and packages/personas/base/** hits — all out of scope this atom.

CONVENTION (matches run_273 atom 1): preserve accepted-ADR recorded decisions and history; reconcile via superseded-pointer / reconciliation notes, NOT by rewriting decision bodies. Never alter correct historical, storage, capability, or generic-working-tree mentions.

MUST NOT BREAK: no code or behavior changes (docs/ADRs only). Run `pnpm typecheck` and any governance/decisions tests (search for tests asserting decisions/README or ADR frontmatter, e.g. `grep -rl decisions packages/**/tests` / `*.test.ts` referencing the decisions dir) and report exit codes — a content edit must not leave a behavior-pinned suite red. If cocoder/decisions/README.md has a one-line summary for any FIXed ADR, leave the summary accurate (only touch it if it itself makes a stale current-truth worktree claim).

DELIVER: the diff plus the full classification table (every grep hit, A/B, reason) and the typecheck/test exit codes.
